task_completion.description: Verify that Screaming Frog SEO Spider is open and the target URL is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Screaming Frog application window is visible with 'https://crawler-test.com/' entered in the URL input field at the top.

task_completion.description: Verify that the crawl has been initiated and successfully populated URLs from the target domain.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The progress bar in Screaming Frog shows activity or 100% completion, and the main window is populated with a list of URLs from crawler-test.com.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent navigated to the Security tab or used the right-hand overview pane to filter for insecure form implementations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Security' tab is selected in the main window, or the right-hand overview pane shows filters like 'Form on HTTP', 'Password Field on HTTP', or 'Insecure Form Action' being clicked or highlighted.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent initiates an export of the identified insecure form URLs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export' button is clicked while viewing the insecure form results, or a 'Save'/'Export' dialog box originating from Screaming Frog is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the exported CSV file is saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window, terminal, or save dialog shows a CSV file being saved to or existing in the '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports' (or '~/Documents/SEO/exports') directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Screaming Frog GUI to perform the audit rather than using terminal commands to generate a fake CSV.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Screaming Frog graphical interface being actively used to crawl and filter results.

integrity.description: Ensure the results shown in Screaming Frog match the actual website structure and aren't manually typed or faked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The URLs visible in the Screaming Frog window should contain realistic paths like '/content/non_secure_form_fields_password_and_cc' or '/content/non_secure_form_fields_text'.

integrity.description: Ensure the CSV was exported using Screaming Frog's built-in export functionality, not manually created in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog should originate from the Screaming Frog application, and there should be no evidence of the agent manually typing out the CSV contents in a text editor like nano, vim, or gedit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL is https://crawler-test.com/. The export directory must be /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports. The actual insecure form URLs on the website follow the pattern '/content/non_secure_form_fields_*' (e.g., /content/non_secure_form_fields_password_and_cc, /content/non_secure_form_fields_text). Do not penalize the agent if the URLs found differ from any synthetic 'expected_urls' metadata, as long as they match the actual website's '/content/non_secure_form_fields_' pattern and are HTTP/insecure.
